blocks.content: That's exactly why I'm telling you this. Don't you understand, Headmaster?

blocks.speaker: Headmaster
blocks.content: Even if he regained his memories, there was no guarantee he'd return to his former self.
Living the same life twice didn't always lead to the same destination.

blocks.speaker: Headmaster
blocks.content: No. My judgment is always correct. No matter how I change, I'll always be me. Last chance. Cast the Photonization Spell.
Sade sighed and nodded.
Now, he could only hope Alpheas's innate nature would restore his original personality.

blocks.speaker: Headmaster
blocks.content: Very well. Please sit here.
Sade brought a chair from the corner and seated Alpheas.
I'll begin. I'll cast the Photonization Spell, but from then on, it's up to you.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Hah! Worrying about me? Just get on with it. Once I get my memories back, I'll teach you a lesson.
Sade channeled his earnest wish into the spell.
As light seeped into Abyss Nova, a slight drowsiness washed over him.
Alpheas slowly closed his eyes and sank into thought.
Time, frozen at 18, began to flow again.
Light and Darkness, Good and Evil (1)
Buried in the underbrush, Canis couldn't move a finger as the shockwave from his abdomen lingered.
After realizing Arin had a talent for telepathy, he had focused on training in dark-physical hybrid magic.
Force absorption was also physical, and thus his Dark Skin's shock absorption was considerable.
An adult's hammer strike would feel like a child's punch against it.

blocks.speaker: Harby
blocks.content: Kekeke. You got me good, Canis.
A murky voice grated on his ears.
Though no one else was in the mountains, the voice was real.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Harby... What the hell hit me?
No idea. But I felt mass. A mass moving at incredible speed struck your gut.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Mass? Light has no mass.

blocks.speaker: Harby
blocks.content: Studying opposing attributes was basic for combat mages, so Canis knew photon theory.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Light has no mass, but it has energy.
Photons in motion indeed had negligible mass, called relativistic mass—like solar wind.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: But that's just mathematical reduction. Like how strong winds feel like being hit with a stick.

blocks.speaker: Harby
blocks.content: Your point stands. But theoretically, it's possible. So not entirely impossible.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: No, something's missing. To deliver this much force, there must be a link between mass and energy. But nothing like that exists.
The voice, Harby, sounded indifferent.

blocks.speaker: Harby
blocks.content: Guess you found your match.
Canis didn't reply.
Though distinct entities, he and Harby shared thoughts.
Sensing Canis's irritation, Harby reluctantly conceded.

blocks.speaker: Harby
blocks.content: Impressive. Unique, too. Didn't expect such talent at the magic school.
Finally satisfied, Canis stood.
Accurately gauging an opponent's strength was the first step to victory.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Can we win, Harby?

blocks.speaker: Harby
blocks.content: Kekeke. Canis, if you wish...
The eerie voice seeped into the darkness.

blocks.speaker: Harby
blocks.content: There's no enemy we can't defeat.
Canis dragged his abnormally long shadow as he returned to the mountaintop where Shirone waited.
Shirone stared at the forest's darkness for a long time.
Though unsure if Canis had cast Dark Skin, his retina registered a warning signal.
Canis emerged, parting the trees.
As Shirone's suspicion was confirmed, Neid frowned.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Dammit. He's really unharmed.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Neid. The sun's set.
With nightfall, dark magic's power would peak.
Iruki cast an illumination spell.
Though dim compared to Fire Sun, Neid amplified it.
Two light sources glowed ten meters above.
But given mages' speed, a full-frontal battle was still impractical.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Tch. Still too dark.
At that moment, Canis cast an illumination spell.
The light magic from a dark mage unsettled the two.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What's he thinking? Illumination magic?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: What's the problem? All schools learn photon theory. It's essential for teleportation.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That's not the point! Light is a dark mage's weakness! Why would he weaken himself?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Hahaha! Hahahaha!
Canis laughed uproariously.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Magic school graduates are so dumb and rigid. No magic is without weaknesses. What matters is compensating for them. This proves it. Arin, fire another.
Even the cautious Arin complied without hesitation.
With four light sources, the brightness resembled daylight.
But unsatisfied, Canis turned to Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Hey, why not fire one too? I'm waiting.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What? You can't? Unbelievable. A light mage who can't cast illumination magic.
Occasionally, such cases existed—short magic threads excelling in one spell through sheer luck.
They might shine in a circus, but magic was killing art.
In battle, where psychology and tactics reigned, specialists like Shirone were easy prey.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: I misjudged you. Thought you'd be a worthy opponent, but you're just a half-baked brat.
As Canis spoke, a light orb burst above Shirone's head.
Its brilliance dwarfed the others.
Neid and Iruki gaped.
Shirone had cast illumination magic without learning it.
His insight was limitless, but his anger was palpable.
Shirone stepped into Canis's Spirit Zone.
He couldn't forgive the man who took the school hostage while preaching about a 'good match.'

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Happy now? I played along. Do your worst.
Canis reconsidered.
Not just a circus act. But did it matter? No matter how strong the opponent, he was stronger.
This was the perfect stage to prove it.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Anything? You'll regret those words.
Casting the Power of Darkness, shadows surged toward Shirone like a torrent.
Shirone soared, photons glowing in his palm.
Another predictable attack.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Just bluffing to piss me off?
Shirone fired a Photon Cannon.
Canis vanished into his shadow—nothing new, just textbook movement magic.
Bombarding the mole-like Canis, the mountaintop was devastated.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Got you!
Analyzing Canis's patterns, Shirone predicted his reappearance and fired.
The Photon Cannon struck as Canis emerged—inescapable, and too powerful for Dark Skin to block.
As all seemed lost, a shadow rose from Canis's feet—elongated like a human shadow—and caught the Photon Cannon with its hands and abdomen.
The photons trapped between its palms were absorbed with a pop.
Shocked, Shirone landed.
The shadow had destroyed the Photon Cannon.
Darkness had overpowered light.

blocks.speaker: Harvest
blocks.content: Kekeke, flashy entrance. How was I?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: How is this possible? Darkness swallowing light...

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: No matter how strong the dark power, it shouldn't suppress light.
Like lighting a torch only for the surroundings to remain dark.
The monstrous shadow, connected to Canis, moved unhindered despite the illumination.
Its face was small, but its shoulders and chest were massive.
Its waist was slender like a panther's, and its arms were long like an ape's.
Its palms were shield-wide, fingers blade-thin.
The unnatural proportions marked it as artificial.

blocks.speaker: Harvest
blocks.content: Kekeke, told you I was no joke. Hey, brat. Your photons were delicious.
The shadow spoke, leaving Shirone dumbfounded.
Its use of 'photons' instead of 'light' hinted at intelligence.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: The essence of dark magic. The ultimate arcane organism—Harvest.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Arcanic organism?
The pinnacle of alchemy and arcane engineering.
Seeing what was only in textbooks was surreal.
Moreover, no book had mentioned an intelligent one.

blocks.speaker: Harvest
blocks.content: Kekeke! He's totally stunned! What's with this guy, like a bumpkin seeing magic beasts for the first time?
Harvest laughed, shoulders shaking.
A frivolous, eccentric monster? No—a being both profoundly human and transcendent.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Harvest is immune to light. And linked to my life. As long as I live, Harby won't perish.

blocks.speaker: Harvest
blocks.content: Yep, yep! We're two but one! One but two! Though I'm way better-looking! Seriously! Wish I could show you. Kehehehe!
Harvest joked, but Shirone had no time for it.
Immunity to light erased dark mages' sole weakness.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Can I... win?
No external help was coming, and Alpheas's whereabouts were unknown.
Hundreds of lives depended on those present.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Canis relished Shirone's dismay.
Revealing Harvest after illumination magic was masterful strategy.
Harvest's playful demeanor was an act—it was far more intelligent than Canis, having inherited Akein's knowledge.
Even now, it exchanged brainwave patterns with Canis.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: - Canis. Let's move into the forest. We're at a disadvantage here.

blocks.speaker: Harvest
blocks.content: - Will they follow?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: - They're the desperate ones. Plus, Lucas is strong. Getting him to act would boost our side.

blocks.speaker: Harvest
blocks.content: - I don't like it. Don't want help from that bastard. Would've fought earlier if you hadn't stopped me.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: - It's for you, Canis. He's strong and not stupid. In raw power, you're outmatched. Can't let you get dragged into petty squabbles.
Lucas was another face of the bullies who tormented Canis in Radum.
The thought of relying on him made his blood boil.
